title: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 30 10 OVER
wikitext:
[]: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0 [OVER!!!]             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 01, 2020 2:57 pm The Spoiler Policy is in effect beginning 14:00 UTC 7-Mar-2021, advanced one hour in order to test subforum password protection. (7/7) The Spoiler Policy is scheduled to end on Monday 23-Aug-2021. Any further changes to the scheduled end will be noted here. The password for the 3.0 + 1.0 subforum is one word, no caps - spoilmerotten (8/30) The Spoiler Policy is now history, folks. Sayonara, Subete no Spoiler Policy! UPDATE 7-7-2021: Scheduled closure date added. UPDATE 8-30-2021: The Spoiler Policy is now history. UPDATE 26-Feb-2021: Due to the announcement of the 8 March release date, this spoiler policy will go into effect at 15:00 UTC 7-Mar-2021. However, in case of content leaks or piracy (see point 1g.) this policy will be implemented suddenly and without prior notice. 1. All spoilers for the movies are to be confined to the 3.0 + 1.0 subforum only . Spoiler material for the movie does not include content available in pre-release social media, official movie trailers or the 10-minute Avant preview, since those are readily accessible. (The 12-minute premiere version of Avant 1, however, is a spoiler, and comes under this policy.) 1a. ALL 3.0 + 1.0 material after the Avant preview and all discussion about it as well as speculation about what lies beyond (if anything) goes in the 3.0 + 1.0 sub-forum. NO EXCEPTIONS. This includes the Evangelion 3.0 (-120 min) supplemental manga. 1b. The 3.0 + 1.0 subforum is password-protected (beginning January, sooner in case of a leak) so that only those users, moderators and administrators who log in will be able to see its content. The password, when set, will be publicly visible in the forum's description and anyone who wants to go in can do so. The password-protection means that users who don't log in will be unable to visit that subforum and unable to find thread titles from that subforum on the "search", "new post" or "unread post" functions. 1c. Threads dedicated to discussing spoiler material are restricted to the 3.0 + 1.0 subforum only . Inside the 3.0 + 1.0 subforum, spoilers may be unmarked in all threads and thread titles may contain spoilers. Do not go into the 3.0 + 1.0 subforum if you want to stay spoiler-free. 1d. Do not make an avatar or signature from any 3.0 + 1.0 material outside of the official pre-01/22 movie trailers, the Avant preview, or promotional materials readily available prior to the theatrical release, pre-01/22. 1e. Spoilery fanart and cosplay images for 3.0 + 1.0 go in this thread , see the first post for additional rules and clarifications. Fanart and cosplay based on pre-release materials go into the regular fanart and cosplay threads, but be warned that any fanart or cosplay that reveals clear spoilers will be treated as spoilers and deleted, and the offenders treated in accordance with the existing spoiler policy. 1f. Any 3.0 + 1.0 fanfiction goes into the spoiler subforum until the spoiler policy is lifted. 1g. As stated in the forum rules, don't ask for or provide links to illegally hosted copyrighted material. This includes Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0 camrips or leaks, including the online 3.0 + 1.0 premiere video . Links will be deleted on sight. Violators will get a summary one-week ban at the very least, no exceptions, with the possibility of a longer ban if board staff deem it applicable. We have zero tolerance for digital piracy , and any repeat attempts at posting illegally hosted material, knowingly flouting the anti-piracy rule , or joining the forum for the sole purpose of selling or distributing illegally obtained 3.0 + 1.0 media on this forum will result in a permanent ban. 1h. If you think you see a spoiler, CONTACT THE STAFF AND DO NOT CALL IT OUT IN-THREAD . Making a public fuss about a non-spoiler can be a far bigger spoiler than the original post itself. For example, calling out a supposed "3.0 spoiler image" in public revealed a spoiler while complaining about an entirely non-spoilery image . 2. Regular, spoiler-free discussion of the previous Rebuild of Evangelion movies may continue in the Rebuild subforum, business as usual. However, in case you want to post post pertinent, non-gratuitous spoiler material (where it makes a meaningful contribution to the discussion and has been explicitly requested) in the body of a post anywhere outside of the 3.0 + 1.0 subforum, you must minimize its legibility in one of two ways - 2a. use spoiler tags to hide the text. The examples below will not be visible unless you click "show"- SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Joseki » Tue Dec 01, 2020 3:11 pm This thread existing made me hyped, even if I will hate every single spoiler thread on this forum lol

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Dec 01, 2020 3:48 pm Get HYPED! HY YY YY YY YY YY YY PE D! !!

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Dec 01, 2020 5:16 pm Thanks for implementing this :) When will this be lifted? IIRC with 3.0 it was some weeks after the Japanese BD came out?

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 01, 2020 5:35 pm Oh, it's not implemented yet . We're setting it up so that everyone knows what the rules are in case we need to implement it in a hurry. Hopefully it'll be implemented as scheduled, 22 January. We have no idea when Thrice Upon A Time will be widely available outside of Japan. If the pandemic causes it to be released to BD or streaming services early, we might very well have it lifted sooner than you think. We'll know for sure in the coming months. For now, do be patient. There's plenty to look forward to.

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Line » Tue Dec 01, 2020 8:18 pm Oh so do that's mean the Rei ":o" face isn't allowed? Because, I actually made a drawing of it that's I posted on a thread

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Dec 02, 2020 1:05 am Spoiler fanart and cosplay thread is up, and I've updated the policy accordingly. thread/20429/Evangelion-30-10-Spoiler-Fanart-and-Cosplay/ Line wrote: Oh so do that's mean the Rei ":o" face isn't allowed? Because, I actually made a drawing of it that's I posted on a thread

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Wed Dec 02, 2020 6:56 am Great! I believe that policy is sufficient. I don't know if I'll resist without looking at the spoilers: I'm curious.

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Dec 02, 2020 7:17 am Feel free to take a look; just don't spoil it for anyone else!

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 02, 2020 7:45 am Line wrote: Oh so do that's mean the Rei ":o" face isn't allowed? Because, I actually made a drawing of it that's I posted on a thread

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Derantor » Wed Dec 02, 2020 10:09 am Small detail: "Q and Beyond" should be changed to whatever the spoiler forum is called right now - or do you intend to create a new subsection, @Ursus?

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Dec 02, 2020 2:17 pm Look again. :P

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by dzzthink » Thu Dec 03, 2020 7:15 pm I was wondering will there be anyone (a moderator perhaps) who will be able to watch the full movie early after release or maybe willing to spoil it for themselves. That person can then monitor posts and delete anything containing spoiler content. Your sacrifice will be greatly appreciated. Come to think of it how will the moderator be able to stop themselves from seeing spoiler content? I imagine spoilers do come up every now and then

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Derantor » Thu Dec 03, 2020 7:33 pm Moderators (and Admins) who are willing to see spoilers will take care of enforcing the spoiler policy. A few have already said they'd be willing to have the movie spoiled; I'll be one of them, when the time comes. As for not seeing spoilered content: if I got it right, the spoiler forum will be password protected. Only people who log in will see spoiler content; that should go for moderators as well, but Ursus knows better and will correct me if I'm wrong.

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by dzzthink » Thu Dec 03, 2020 7:43 pm Luke 6:38 "Give, and it will be given to you. Good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, will be put into your lap. For with the measure you use it will be measured back to you.” (Thanks a lot)

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Dec 10, 2020 1:38 pm Derantor wrote: As for not seeing spoilered content: if I got it right, the spoiler forum will be password protected. Only people who log in will see spoiler content; that should go for moderators as well, but Ursus knows better and will correct me if I'm wrong.

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Joseki » Thu Dec 24, 2020 9:17 am How does 3.333 fit into the spoiler policy?

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Dec 24, 2020 12:58 pm Good question. I've put it to the other staff, we'll get back to you in a while. My personal take is that it shouldn't come under the spoiler policy at all unless you consider the changes to plugsuit coloration to be spoilers. Of course, I would be willing to change my mind if 3.333 had new plot-relevant content like restored deleted scenes, for instance.

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Dec 24, 2020 4:52 pm ∆ Yeah, I share that view. It's of interest to discuss "visual updates", but new scenes can be considered new work, even if it exists within an existing film.

[]: Re: Spoiler Policy for Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by cyharding » Thu Dec 24, 2020 8:04 pm You would think we would have heard if there are new scenes by now, so I think it's okay.
